title: From news headlines to exposing shared risks: Strengthening Transboundary Climate and Disaster Reporting in Nepal and India
content: Rivers flowing from the Himalaya carried floodwaters downstream into India’s Bihar state affecting between 1.757 million and 4.5 million peoplee. These cascading effects illustrate how communities along shared river systems face interconnected risks

title: Mainstreaming disaster resilience into infrastructure projects: Insights from infrastructure sectors in India
content: The study addresses growing disaster risks to infrastructure caused by climate change and extreme weather, which generate significant economic losses globally and in India.
snippet: The study addresses growing disaster risks to infrastructure caused by climate change and extreme weather, which generate significant economic losses globally and in India.

title: Financial inclusion as a driver of earthquake recovery and resilience in Myanmar
content: Financial inclusion is not only a pathway out of poverty, but also a critical tool for disaster risk reduction, as shown by a project of financial resilience for women in Myanmar.
snippet: Financial inclusion is not only a pathway out of poverty, but also a critical tool for disaster risk reduction, as shown by a project of financial resilience for women in Myanmar.

title: Cool cities lab
content: It is an open-source data platform to help cities better understand and respond to urban heat risks.  The platform provides city officials with access to globally available, high-resolution data to assess heat exposure at a hyperlocal level
snippet: It is an open-source data platform to help cities better understand and respond to urban heat risks. The platform provides city officials with access to globally available, high-resolution data to ass

title: Time to be systemic: integrating and mainstreaming climate mitigation and adaptation across all scales
content: This perspective argues for a systemic approach by integrating mitigation and adaptation by default into every policy, budget, and planning decision, from local neighbourhoods to national governments.
snippet: This perspective argues for a systemic approach by integrating mitigation and adaptation by default into every policy, budget, and planning decision, from local neighbourhoods to national governments.

title: How corporations operationalize climate-related risks and opportunities: Evidence from qualitative expert interviews
content: Drawing on eight semi-structured interviews with sustainability professionals across multiple industries, the research explores how firms interpret and operationalize disclosure requirements in practice
snippet: Drawing on eight semi-structured interviews with sustainability professionals across multiple industries, the research explores how firms interpret and operationalize disclosure requirements in practi

title: Beyond the harvest: Uncovering the hidden risks driving poverty and hunger in developing economies
content: When evaluating national vulnerability, analysts often look at aggregate GDP. The study confirmed that in most of the analyzed developing countries, overall GDP volatility is predominantly driven by domestic agricultural shocks.

title: Democracy meets extreme heat: India’s elections in a warming climate
content: Campaigning for votes when temperatures soar is dangerous, but many say India is in denial about the problem
snippet: Campaigning for votes when temperatures soar is dangerous, but many say India is in denial about the problem

title: Gender-differentiated adaptation and social protection: Applications and future directions for analysis and policy
content: This workshop will explore methods for measuring climate shocks, from rainfall variability to floods and heatwaves, followed by applications that focus on how weather shocks affect poor rural households and the role of social protection.

title: Advancing Heat Risk Governance and Action: Responding to the UN Secretary-General’s Call to Action on Extreme Heat
content: This webinar aims to build attendees’ understanding of integrated extreme heat risk governance and investment, and share real-world insights from countries across different governance levels.
snippet: This webinar aims to build attendees’ understanding of integrated extreme heat risk governance and investment, and share real-world insights from countries across different governance levels.

title: Brunei: Disaster management reference handbook 2026
content: Brunei is a wealthy, well-governed small nation on Borneo with a robust domestic disaster management system that rarely needs international support, yet remains an active contributor to regional disaster response through ASEAN frameworks.

title: Seismic ‘whiplash’ – research shows what happens when earthquakes stop suddenly
content: The magnitude of an earthquake depends on how far a rupture travels along a fault line before it stops. For the first time, scientists have now directly observed how a large earthquake comes to a halt.
snippet: The magnitude of an earthquake depends on how far a rupture travels along a fault line before it stops. For the first time, scientists have now directly observed how a large earthquake comes to a halt

title: Countries will suffer significant economic damage over next 25 years without investments in resilience against climate change impacts
content: Climate change impacts are already damaging economies, particularly in lower-income countries, and finance ministries should prioritise investments in adaptation and resilience to limit further harm.
snippet: Climate change impacts are already damaging economies, particularly in lower-income countries, and finance ministries should prioritise investments in adaptation and resilience to limit further harm.

title: The macroeconomic case for investing in climate adaptation
content: This report provides a groundbreaking new synthesis of the economic and fiscal risks arising from physical climate change and the economic case for investing in adaptation to climate impacts.
snippet: This report provides a groundbreaking new synthesis of the economic and fiscal risks arising from physical climate change and the economic case for investing in adaptation to climate impacts.

title: Why financing recovery matters before disasters strike: lessons from Sri Lanka
content: When disasters strike, they do more than damage homes and infrastructure—they test how quickly countries can mobilize finance, coordinate institutions, and turn “build back better” from aspiration into action.

title: Tracing cascading economic impacts of volcanic disruption through regional value chains: Implications for disaster risk reduction
content: This study examines the cascading economic consequences of a volcanic disruption scenario in the Kawerau District of New Zealand's Bay of Plenty, a nationally significant hub for paper manufacturing.
snippet: This study examines the cascading economic consequences of a volcanic disruption scenario in the Kawerau District of New Zealand's Bay of Plenty, a nationally significant hub for paper manufacturing.

title: Resilience and response: Understanding community’s policy perspectives on flood and erosion in Assam
content: The study conducts a stakeholder perception analysis using a qualitative approach through Focus Group Discussions (FGDs) and semi-structured and structured interviews with flood-prone communities of Barpeta, Dibrugarh, and Majuli districts in Assam.

title: Major fires in Indonesian Borneo are possible under all ENSO phases
content: In this study the authors analysed a large initialised ensemble of global climate simulations to demonstrate that although the risk of severe fires that level or surpass those observed in 1997 and 2015 is strongly boosted by an El Niño by a factor of 2.7.

title: Ocean warming weakens the sea–land breeze in coastal megacities
content: This paper simulates sea–land breeze (SLB) evolution across 18 major coastal megacities under varying sea-surface temperatures (SST).
snippet: This paper simulates sea–land breeze (SLB) evolution across 18 major coastal megacities under varying sea-surface temperatures (SST).

title: These eight coastal cities sit on America's flood front line, and AI shows why
content: New York, New Orleans and Miami are among the eight cities along the US Gulf and Atlantic coasts facing the highest flood risk, according to a study published in Science Advances.
snippet: New York, New Orleans and Miami are among the eight cities along the US Gulf and Atlantic coasts facing the highest flood risk, according to a study published in Science Advances.

title: Mapping potential funding sources for ecosystem-based adaptation insurance premiums
content: This study presents an exploratory, legitimate, and fiscal analysis of potential public financing pathways that can enable LGUs to pay premiums for parametric insurance schemes aimed at protecting and restoring mangrove and marine ecosystems.

title: Why the Southeast is burning – extreme drought is only part of the reason
content: As global temperatures rise, the frequency of drought conditions in the Southeast will increase. This, in combination with less soil moisture content in the summer, could be conducive for increased wildfire activity.

title: India plans contract overhaul to embed disaster resilience as report flags Rs 1.81 lakh crore infrastructure exposure
content: As climate risks intensify, resilience is no longer an optional add-on but a core requirement to ensure that infrastructure systems remain functional, financially viable, and sustainable, says report
snippet: As climate risks intensify, resilience is no longer an optional add-on but a core requirement to ensure that infrastructure systems remain functional, financially viable, and sustainable, says report

title: 2025 was EU’s most destructive wildfire season on record
content: Fires started early in the year and intensified during summer heatwaves.
snippet: Fires started early in the year and intensified during summer heatwaves.

title: Lancet Europe report shows increase in heat‑attributable deaths and ballooning energy costs from fossil fuels
content: Nearly every monitored European region (99.6 percent) has seen an increase in heat‑attributable deaths over the past decade according to the report, to which researchers from the Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research (PIK) contributed.

title: Overheating: is the UK failing to respond to the impacts of climate change?

title: Climate Solutions Symposium 2026
content: Join the 2026 Climate Solutions Symposium, May 18–20, to explore how climate change is shaping communities and ecosystems in Pennsylvania and beyond.
snippet: Join the 2026 Climate Solutions Symposium, May 18–20, to explore how climate change is shaping communities and ecosystems in Pennsylvania and beyond.

title: Extreme heat risk governance framework and toolkit
content: United Nations Secretary-General has identified extreme heat as a priority climate challenge, calling for urgent, coordinated action to enhance international cooperation across sectors and domains.
snippet: United Nations Secretary-General has identified extreme heat as a priority climate challenge, calling for urgent, coordinated action to enhance international cooperation across sectors and domains.

title: Capturing invisible heat: redefining how we measure losses
content: Heat is one of the deadliest climate hazards, yet its true impact remains difficult to quantify. Heat rarely leaves visible destruction, and its toll is often hidden in health statistics, economic losses, and gradual declines in well-being.
snippet: Heat is one of the deadliest climate hazards, yet its true impact remains difficult to quantify. Heat rarely leaves visible destruction, and its toll is often hidden in health statistics, economic los

title: The planet under heat stress: impacts beyond people and assets
content: Contemporary discussions on climate change and extreme heat remain largely anthropocentric, centring human health, infrastructure, and economic disruption while obscuring broader systemic impacts.
snippet: Contemporary discussions on climate change and extreme heat remain largely anthropocentric, centring human health, infrastructure, and economic disruption while obscuring broader systemic impacts.

title: Beyond the thermometer: rethinking how we measure heat risk
content: Extreme heat cannot be managed with incomplete measurements. A single thermometer reading cannot capture humidity, night-time heat persistence, cumulative exposure, or the physiological limits of different populations.
snippet: Extreme heat cannot be managed with incomplete measurements. A single thermometer reading cannot capture humidity, night-time heat persistence, cumulative exposure, or the physiological limits of diff

title: Private sector-led capacity building: ARISE Philippines launches 2026 hands-only CPR campaign
content: ARISE Philippines has commenced its 2026 Hands-Only CPR Campaign, reinforcing private sector engagement in disaster risk reduction (DRR) and contributing to national resilience-building efforts.
snippet: ARISE Philippines has commenced its 2026 Hands-Only CPR Campaign, reinforcing private sector engagement in disaster risk reduction (DRR) and contributing to national resilience-building efforts.

title: Astana Recognized as Central Asia’s First MCR2030 Resilience Hub
content: Astana has been recognized as the first Making Cities Resilient 2030 (MCR2030) Resilience Hub in Central Asia.
snippet: Astana has been recognized as the first Making Cities Resilient 2030 (MCR2030) Resilience Hub in Central Asia.

title: LGBTIQ+ people in disaster risk reduction
content: Disasters do not affect everyone equally. LGBTQI+ people face specific and often overlooked risks in disasters and climate crises.
snippet: Disasters do not affect everyone equally. LGBTQI+ people face specific and often overlooked risks in disasters and climate crises.

title: ESA funds Polish radio telescope to monitor solar outbursts
content: A station in Poland has begun monitoring the Sun for the early warning signs of disruptive space weather phenomena. The six-metre radio dish, known as ROSIE, will contribute to the protection of key European infrastructure in orbit and on Earth.

title: Cities and countries warming fast, updated climate stripes show
content: The updated graphics, which now include an additional stripe to represent temperatures from 2025, show the rapid impact that global warming is having on individual nations and regions.
snippet: The updated graphics, which now include an additional stripe to represent temperatures from 2025, show the rapid impact that global warming is having on individual nations and regions.

title: It wasn't just water: The hidden force inside Japan's 2011 tsunami changed everything
content: Mud-rich coastlines could face a greater tsunami risk, at least that may have been the case for the 2011 Tōhoku-oki tsunami. Mud may have made the catastrophic ocean waves more destructive than they might otherwise have been.

title: Lismore unveils new flood pumps to enhance city protection and resilience
content: In Lismore, New South Wales, flood pumps are replacing old stormwater removal units previously powered by tractors.
snippet: In Lismore, New South Wales, flood pumps are replacing old stormwater removal units previously powered by tractors.
